text.title: OpenAI Embeddings — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules that every embedding pipeline must enforce. Source: OpenAI Embeddings guide, RAG retrieval research, internal failure logs (2026).

rule.statement: Pin both `model` (e.g., `text-embedding-3-small`) and `dimensions` (e.g., 1536) in a single config consumed by both ingestion and query paths. Mismatch fails the startup smoke test.

rationale.source: OpenAI Embeddings guide; production failure logs

rule.rationale: Mixing model or dimensions between ingestion and query produces random similarity scores with no error — the silent-failure mode is the worst kind.

rule.statement: Chunk documents at semantic boundaries (paragraphs, sections) with ~10% overlap. Chunk before embed; never feed inputs > 8191 tokens raw.

rationale.source: RAG retrieval research; OpenAI Embeddings docs

rule.rationale: Fixed-token chunking loses semantic coherence; overlap prevents boundary misses; silent truncation produces partial embeddings.

rule.statement: Persist both the embedding vector and the original chunk text in the vector store. Cache embeddings for unchanged content — never re-embed static text.
rule.rationale: Embeddings cannot be reversed; storing only vectors makes search results unreturnable. Re-embedding static content wastes spend.

rule.statement: For RAG retrieval, embed an expanded / rewritten query (LLM-generated), not the raw user message — short or ambiguous inputs lose recall otherwise.
rule.rationale: Short queries embed into sparse regions of the vector space; expansion enriches them.

rule.statement: Every Batch API request carries a meaningful `custom_id` (e.g., `doc_{id}_chunk_{n}`). Results are reconstructed by `custom_id` — never by output line order.
rule.rationale: Batch results return in arbitrary order; positional assumptions misattribute embeddings silently.

rule.statement: Do not embed sensitive PII (names, emails, SSNs, internal IDs) into a multi-tenant or shared vector store. Use per-tenant indices or redact pre-embed.
rule.rationale: Nearest-neighbor queries on a shared store let an adversary extract PII by probing with known fragments.
